check_event_support.note: Mandiant (Feb 2013) exposes 'Unit 61398' = '2nd Bureau of the PLA GSD 3rd Department' at the Pudong, Shanghai facility, 'cyber espionage... since at least 2006' (matches start 2006). DOJ (19 May 2014) charges five named Unit 61398 officers — 'first ever charges against a state actor for this type of hacking.'

check_attribution_support.note: Mandiant high ('APT1 is likely government-sponsored... is Unit 61398'); DOJ definitive on the five named officers. High/concur supported.

check_targets_match.note: DOJ names Westinghouse, U.S. Steel, Alcoa, SolarWorld (+ ATI, USW). All four primary targets present; sectors nuclear/metals/solar match.

check_doctrine_honest.note: cn/mic2025 attested to the 2014 DOJ indictment is ANACHRONISTIC — MIC2025 was announced in 2015, so the indictment cannot name it; the link's own reasoning concedes the victims only 'became MIC2025 pillars two years later' under the '12th Five-Year Plan.' Downgrade attested to strongly_inferred (a 12th-Five-Year-Plan attested link would be defensible — Mandiant names it). cn/mcf (strongly_inferred) is honest.

draft_qc_note: Attribution/targets solid. Downgrade cn/mic2025 from attested to strongly_inferred — MIC2025 postdates the 2014 indictment and cannot be named by it; consider re-keying the attested link to the 12th Five-Year Plan (named in the Mandiant report).

check_source_resolves: ok: ESET WeLiveSecurity (40k) on-target

check_event_support.note: ESET (05 Jun 2025): BladedFeline active since 2017, KRG + Iraqi-government + Uzbekistan-telecom victims; Whisper/Shahmaran/PrimeCache/Flog/Laret/Pinar toolkit; OilRig linkage via VideoSRV/RDAT recovery + PrimeCache-RDAT code similarity; activity Sept 2017 to March 2024 tool uploads. All confirmed.

check_attribution_support.note: ESET assesses 'with medium confidence that BladedFeline is a subgroup of OilRig' and calls it 'Iran-aligned' — it never names MOIS. actor_id ir/mois/bladedfeline asserts MOIS specifically, which the source does not support (moderate/concur confidence label itself matches ESET's 'medium confidence').

check_targets_match.note: Uzbekistan telecom (uz) and Iraqi government (iq) confirmed; KRG is the central long-running victim but has no dedicated target entry — coding KRG diplomats under foreign-ministry (iq) is geographically reasonable but underrepresents KRG's centrality.

check_doctrine_honest.note: ir/forward-defense is plausible/inference (attesting null) — no attestation claimed; reasonable given ESET's description of collection across Iran's periphery to 'counter the influence of Western governments.'

draft_qc_note: PROVISIONAL header valid. Reconcile actor_id ir/mois/bladedfeline — ESET says only 'Iran-aligned'/medium-confidence OilRig subgroup, not MOIS. Consider adding a KRG-specific target entry (KRG is the primary victim).

check_source_resolves: flag: CISA AA21-116A (16.2k) usable; NCSC-UK 'Further TTPs' source is EMPTY (0 chars)

check_event_support.note: AA21-116A confirms SVR attribution and references the 15 Apr 2021 WH statement, and supports start 2018 ('Beginning in 2018, the FBI observed the SVR shift...'). But the advisory does NOT mention the OFAC six-company sanctions or the 10-diplomat expulsion (real but external), and the 'first NCSC technical attribution by name' claim relies on the empty NCSC source.

check_attribution_support.note: SVR-by-name attribution solid ('Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR)... APT 29, the Dukes, CozyBear'). BUT AA21-116A is authored by FBI/DHS/CISA — NSA and NCSC-UK are NOT bylined on THIS advisory; the org coding 'CISA + FBI + NSA + NCSC-UK' overstates this document's authorship.

check_targets_match.note: 'SVR primarily targets government networks, think tank and policy analysis organizations, and information technology companies' supports sectors/government + sectors/ict primary.

check_doctrine_honest.note: uk/ncs-2016 attested to a US CISA/FBI/DHS technical advisory that never mentions the UK National Cyber Security Strategy 2016 or any 'deter' pillar — it is a TTP/network-defense document. Attested cannot stand; downgrade to strongly_inferred. Compounding: the NCSC-UK source that could support a UK-doctrine framing is empty.

draft_qc_note: Re-fetch the empty NCSC-UK source. Downgrade uk/ncs-2016 from attested to strongly_inferred — the US advisory does not name the UK strategy or a deter pillar. Correct the attribution byline (AA21-116A is FBI/DHS/CISA, not NSA/NCSC-UK).
